title: Arnoglossum maritimum
taxonomy: Arnoglossum maritimum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it alongside other flowering plants that produce daisy-like flowers. At the genus level, Arnoglossum maritimum is part of the genus Arnoglossum, which comprises a group of flowering plants native to North America. The species is further classified within the genus, highlighting its distinct characteristics and traits. Overall, its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Arnoglossum runcinatum (previously known as Arnoglossum maritimum) is actually a synonym for Eryngium maritimum. Arnoglossum maritimum does not appear to be a valid plant name. However, Eryngium maritimum, also known as Sea Holly, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and roots have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's sap is also said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat wounds and skin irritations.
